text.tspan: When things go wrong — and how to bring them back.
The pan was too hot. Residual heat cooked the yolks
into curds before they could emulsify.
Let the pan sit longer next run, or splash a
tablespoon of cool pasta water before adding the
The emulsion lost moisture — too much fat
absorbed by the pasta, or the pan was too cold.
Slowly stir in reserved pasta starch water, one
tablespoon at a time, off the heat, until silken.
The Pecorino was grated too coarsely, or it was
tossed onto dry pasta before the fat was added.
Use a microplane, and always mix the cheese with
